items.rationale: His foundational, self-identified conviction — decentralization lets participants own and operate networks rather than be at their mercy; he frames himself as a 'zealot' and continues cheering builders through the 2022 downturn.
items.age_note: Consistent from 2021-08 through 2022-11; most recent restatement 2022-11-11, so current not stale.

citations.quote: gm to everyone building and supporting decentralized networks
someone once called me a zealot about decentralization i thought this was high praise, obviously a complement
Crypto matters because participants can own and operate a network instead of being at its mercy

items.rationale: He repeatedly insists ZKPs are underrated and transformational, and is actively diligencing scaled L1 privacy (Aleo) and proving-system overhead — a durable, growing interest.

citations.quote: i still think people underestimate how transformational zero knowledge proofs are
It's fascinating to see scaled L1 privacy with ZKPs!
You should read @SuccinctJT's blog... a really insightful post on how to measure the overhead of proving systems

items.rationale: Self-identifies with cypherpunks, led/championed privacy investments (Nym at the networking layer), and curates a list of privacy projects he admires; treats network-level privacy as essential, not optional.

citations.quote: @nymproject will enable essential network level privacy for crypto, web3, and the broader Internet
DMs are always open for cypherpunks

items.rationale: Led a16z's $25M seed into Entropy; sees decentralized custody as the way past 'not your keys, not your crypto,' giving permissionless secure access and policy-defined wallets — one of his most concrete, recent high-conviction bets.

citations.quote: Entropy has the potential to bring decentralization to custody, allowing permissionless, secure, and efficient access to almost any token for any user.
With the rise of smart contract wallets, multi-sig, and multi-party compute protocols we can finally advance past “not your keys, not your crypto.”

items.rationale: Led the Morpho investment; views on-chain lending as an essential DeFi primitive and is excited by combining peer-to-peer matching with peer-to-pool to deliver a Pareto rate improvement and expand the design space.
items.age_note: Reaffirmed 2022-11-17 (celebrating Morpho/Instadapp integration), so still current.

citations.quote: We believe on-chain lending is an essential DeFi primitive
Morpho has the potential to significantly lower the cost of decentralized capital and increase capital efficiency across DeFi

items.rationale: Believes truly decentralized games must put logic on-chain and that the 'metagame token' will be the DeFi-governance-token equivalent for games, with player-owned assets and emergent win conditions.
items.age_note: Peak articulation Oct 2021; restated through 2022-04-26, so persisted and lightly refreshed.

citations.quote: if you want truly decentralized crypto games you have to put logic on-chain
I believe the DeFi governance token equivalent for games will become the metagame token.

items.rationale: Treats DAOs as new forms of human coordination (unions, internet-native countries) and argues permissionless, retroactively-compensated contribution is essential to their long-term success.

citations.quote: "permissionless contribution" is an idea i believe is essential for the long term success of daos
What if DAOs are actually Internet native countries decoupled from physical space?

items.rationale: Recurring interest in pushing state to the address level for reputation, credit scoring and underwriting, with a clear design requirement (cost-to-build >> extractable value) — a problem he wants solved.
items.age_note: Idea raised 2021-09/10 and restated 2022-07-25, so persisted and current.

citations.quote: cost to build reputation R >> value that can be extracted given reputation R can this be done efficiently?
would love to see more state pushed to the address level in the form of non-tranferable tokens for things like reputation

items.rationale: Excited to plug multiple curation algorithms onto an open social data stream, reflecting interest in user-controlled feeds and decentralized social graphs.

citations.quote: I’m very excited to plug and play multiple curation algorithms on the same farcaster data stream

items.rationale: Backed LayerZero and is enthusiastic about new 'dimensions of composability,' which he ties to expanding what builders can create across chains.

citations.quote: It's been a privilege working with the @LayerZero_Labs team and we're excited to see more dimensions of composability become possible!

items.rationale: Heavy early enthusiasm for NFTs (generative mints, lock-ups by rarity, on-chain text, the nouns model) as cultural artifacts and coordination primitives.
items.age_note: Dominant theme in 2021; by mid/late 2022 attention shifted toward privacy, custody and DeFi — interest persists but de-emphasized. Last notable mention 2022-08-09.

citations.quote: @0xMasonH has started calling NFTs cultural artifacts and you should too
The nouns model for NFT mints should be more popular imo

items.rationale: When asked what he fears, he points to centralization at the networking layer letting DNS providers and ISPs censor large portions of crypto networks — a chokepoint above the protocol.

citations.quote: centralization at the network level that would allow DNS providers and ISPs to censor large portions of most crypto networks

items.rationale: Worries that with 4844 blobs few validators actually persist data and instead query a small set of RPC providers, which is centralizing.

citations.quote: If only a few nodes actually store blob data it's centralizing

items.rationale: Openly acknowledges decentralization is always less efficient than centralization and asks who bears that cost — a clear-eyed concern about the core tradeoff, not blind boosterism.

citations.quote: Decentralization is always less efficient than centralization. Why is this cost worth paying, and who will bear it?

items.rationale: Flags that correctly verifying a service was actually provided/executed is almost always the hardest problem when building a new decentralized network — an unsolved blocker he wants addressed.

citations.quote: correctly verifying that a service has been provided/executed is almost always the hard problem when building a new decentralized network

items.rationale: Concerned that imprecise 'scissor labels' like DAO and token compress too many distinct things, eroding clarity — echoing how 'blockchain' was misapplied to distributed databases in a prior cycle.

citations.quote: DAO seems to be one of the scissor label... casualties of recent times, much like too many people started calling distributed databases blockchains

items.rationale: Singled out Solana as the venue he'd like to see on-chain game teams choose for the highest-throughput, atomically composable execution environment.
items.age_note: Stated once on 2022-01-14 and never restated; pre-dates the 2022 SOL drawdown and FTX collapse, which he does not address — treat as stale.

citations.quote: it would be great to see some teams chose @solana for the highest throughput atomically composable execution environment

risk_regime.rationale: Despite tweeting through a deep bear market, his posture is relentlessly long-term-constructive: he urges 'indulge optimism' post-vibe-shift, insists 'crypto is still so early,' and keeps deploying capital (Entropy, Morpho) and cheering builders. His optimism is structural conviction in the technology's trajectory rather than tactical price/market timing — he expresses essentially no market-timing or near-term price view.

notes: Wuollet is an a16z crypto investor focused on decentralized protocols/networks, so his 'view' is a builder/VC thesis, not a trading book — almost everything is structural with no tactical price calls. Most-recent (mid-to-late 2022) signal concentrates on privacy/ZKPs, decentralized custody (Entropy), and DeFi lending (Morpho); NFTs and metagame-token themes were louder in 2021 and have faded in emphasis (recorded as persisted with age_notes). Notably, the corpus ends 2022-12-31 with no mention of the FTX collapse (Nov 2022) or broader market stress — his concerns are technical/architectural (network-layer censorship, DA centralization, verification, efficiency cost of decentralization) rather than macro or solvency-driven. Few specific tokens are named; Solana is the only token-level mention and is stale. Conviction scores reflect intensity/centrality to his stated worldview, not certainty of investment outcome.
